steps.title: Identify the central claim
steps.content: The passage's central claim is stated in Fernandes's observation: “these two traditions aren't as distinct as they seem.” This directly reveals the passage's purpose—to challenge a supposed distinction and assert an underlying similarity.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: Answer C directly states the passage's purpose: to assert that private and early public libraries in ancient Rome had an essential similarity. The other choices either address matters the passage doesn't cover (A's overview, D's disappearance) or add unsupported claims (B's excessive influence). That matches choice C — C is correct.
